title: Uragoga nymanniana
intro: Uragoga nymanniana, also known as Uragoga, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, traditionally used in medicine. The health benefits of Uragoga include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been associated with the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Uragoga is commonly used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed as herbal remedies in traditional medicine, particularly in West African countries.
taxonomy: Uragoga nymanniana is classified as a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Uragoga, which is part of the order Magnoliales.
